info.title: RFx Web Services
info.description: Web Services for managing the RFx.

get.summary: Search RFx template for the provided input string.

parameters.name: $searchStr
parameters.description: Search Criteria; 2 or more characters required.

parameters.name: $top
parameters.description: The number of objects to return; default 50; max 1000

parameters.name: $skip
parameters.description: The number of objects to skip in case of paginating through the serach results; default 0

parameters.name: $state
parameters.description: Search RFx Template objects based on state; active or all

post.summary: Get a checkin ticket to upload files to FCS; required for checking in a file to a new document that is not created yet.

parameters.name: $include
parameters.description: Specify sub-resources that should be returned by the web request.  Provide combination of comma separated entries including keywords none & all plus payload specific sub-resources: [!]suppliers, [!]send, [!]attachments, [!]detachAttachments, [!]rfxTemplate, [!]lineitems.  Some entries are enabled by default which are described in the schema section of the response payload.  For more information about refinement, see 6W REST Services Principles - Refinement Section.

post.summary: Create RFQ. Send rfxTemplate data if the expression MakeRFxTeamplateMandatory value is true.

parameters.name: $include
parameters.description: Specify sub-resources that should be returned by the web request.  Provide combination of comma separated entries including keywords none & all plus payload specific sub-resources: [!]suppliers, [!]send, [!]attachments, [!]detachAttachments, [!]rfxTemplate, [!]buyer, [!]lineitems.  Some entries are enabled by default which are described in the schema section of the response payload.  For more information about refinement, see 6W REST Services Principles - Refinement Section.

get.summary: get Line Items connected to RFx.

post.summary: Create and Connect RFx To Line Items.

get.summary: Get Attachments connected to Line Item.
Search RFQs for the provided input string.

parameters.name: searchStr
parameters.description: Search Criteria; 2 or more characters required.

parameters.name: $top
parameters.description: The number of objects to return; default 50; max 1000

parameters.name: $skip
parameters.description: The number of objects to skip in case of paginating through the serach results; default 0

parameters.name: $include
parameters.description: Specify sub-resources that should be returned by the web request.  Provide combination of comma separated entries including keywords none & all plus payload specific sub-resources: [!]suppliers, [!]send, [!]attachments, [!]detachAttachments, [!]rfxTemplate, [!]buyer, [!]lineitems.  Some entries are enabled by default which are described in the schema section of the response payload.  For more information about refinement, see 6W REST Services Principles - Refinement Section.

post.summary: RFx will be moved to Sent state.

owner.description: The owner of the component request

ticketURL.description: The URL to retrieve the file from the FCS serrver.

ticketparamname.description: The Ticket parameter name required by the FCS server.

ticket.description: The ticket paramter value required by the FCS server.

fileName.description: The file name associated with the download ticket in the case one file is being downloaded.

fileNames.description: The file names associated with the download ticket in the case multiple files are being downloaded.

cestamp.description: The Concurrent Engineering (CESTAMP) of the object.

updateAction.description: Specify the sub-resource update action. See 6W Web Service Principles: Data Object Updates

identifier.description: The unique object identifier when an object is represented by a Public Resource URI.

source.description: The object server URL when an object is represented by a Public Resource URI.

relativePath.description: The object resource path when an object is represented by a Public Resource URI.

csrf.description: CSRF object is required to be sent back for update operations and may be provided with read operations.

success.description: The response data when the request succeeds with 200 status code.
Reflects true to indicate successful request.

error-400.description: The response data when the request fails with 400 or 500 error.

error.description: An NLS error message.  In the case of a 400 error, the message is precise.  In the case of 500 error, the message is generic.

internalError.description: The technical reason of the failure - usually associated with a 500 error; this is not an NLS message and reflects the MQL or runtime exception.

error-401.description: The response data when the request fails with 400 or 500 error.

error.description: An NLS error message.  In the case of a 400 error, the message is precise.  In the case of 500 error, the message is generic.

internalError.description: The technical reason of the failure - usually associated with a 500 error; this is not an NLS message and reflects the MQL or runtime exception.

error-403.description: The response data when the request fails with 400 or 500 error.

error.description: An NLS error message.  In the case of a 400 error, the message is precise.  In the case of 500 error, the message is generic.

internalError.description: The technical reason of the failure - usually associated with a 500 error; this is not an NLS message and reflects the MQL or runtime exception.

error-500.description: The response data when the request fails with 400 or 500 error.

error.description: An NLS error message.  In the case of a 400 error, the message is precise.  In the case of 500 error, the message is generic.

internalError.description: The technical reason of the failure - usually associated with a 500 error; this is not an NLS message and reflects the MQL or runtime exception.

SecurityContext.name: SecurityContext
SecurityContext.description: The Security Context setting for the web request.

Accept-Language.name: Accept-Language
Accept-Language.description: Identifies the locale for the web request.

ENO_CSRF_TOKEN.name: ENO_CSRF_TOKEN
ENO_CSRF_TOKEN.description: The ENOVIA CSRF token which is typically required for update operations.  Retrieve using {server}/resources/v1/application/CSRF

fields.name: $fields
fields.description: Specify resource and sub-resource fields that should be returned by the web request  Fields include keywords none & all plus payload fields - see schema response payload.  For more information, see 6W REST Services Principles - Refinement Section
